$comment: Ch 1 — THE ARC, scenario 2. Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. The textbook-classical answer at θ=55° is v ≈ 7.82 m/s (see meta.authorIntent for derivation). The pedagogical rule from the reference scenario is sacred: scenario answers come from real physics formulae.

meta.title: TOO FLAT TO FALL.
meta.subtitle: CH 1 — THE ARC, NO LIFT
meta.authorIntent: The cocky elbow jumper with almost no arc. d=5.0, h_r=2.0, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=55°, v=7.82 m/s. Verification: t = 5.0 / (7.82·cos 55°) = 1.115 s; y(1.115) = 2.0 + 7.82·sin(55°)·1.115 − ½·9.8·1.115² = 3.052 m — matches h_h (err 0.002 m). Pedagogical purpose: prove that a confident-looking release is not the same as a makeable release. A low-angle answer here (θ near 40°) yields a flat shot that approaches the rim from too shallow an angle. The correct answer sits in the high-50s, giving a visible parabola the user can see is more shot-shaped than the baseline. Lesson revealed by contrast: arc isn't just one of several variables, it's the variable.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. A flat shot has no margin.
subheadVariants.2: Still short. The arc needs more height.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. The arc never got over the lip.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Push the arc up.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. The trajectory is too low.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Pushed it too hard for that angle.
subheadVariants.3+: Back iron again. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long. The lift was right, the power wasn't.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. The release never climbed.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=55°, v=7.82 m/s. Visibly the 'right shape' — a clear arc that crests well above the rim before settling. Compare to the baseline answer (θ=52°, v=7.52) — same release, slightly more distance, demands a higher arc.

hints.body: Topic: projectile motion. Same equations as the free throw, but the release is lower and the distance is longer.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Reaches the rim when x = d and y = h_h.
Eliminate t with t = d/(v·cos θ). Substitute into y. Try θ near 55° and solve for v.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.82 m/s.

intro.headline: TOO FLAT TO FALL.
intro.subhead: A confident elbow release with almost no rise.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Pushed the arc up. Margin restored.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Front iron, then in. Generous bounce.

miss.headline: MISSED.

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. The arc never got over the lip.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Pushed it too hard for that angle.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short. A flat shot has no margin.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long. The lift was right, the power wasn't.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball. The release never climbed.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 55°, hold v near 7.8.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 55°, cut v by ~0.5.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: lift θ to 55°, push v above 7.8.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 55°, cut v by ~1.0.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
